signals.reasoning: BTC is firm but still lacks a meaningful directional edge, so it remains a benchmark hold under the red macro regime.
ETH is flat with no compelling positioning imbalance, which keeps it out of the trade bucket despite the clear veto state.
SOL is stable, but the mechanical board is too muted to justify a fresh long or short here.
CHILLGUY is extended after another strong surge, funding is positive, and execution quality is weak, which makes it cleaner to fade than to chase.
TAO remains one of the better shorts because price is still breaking lower and the rebound support is too weak to offset the broader downside structure.
TNSR is still the strongest long because funding remains materially negative, squeeze risk is high, and the mechanical board keeps it in full trade territory.
MON has upside momentum but it looks too extended and too noisy to justify a clean entry in either direction right now.
DASH has strong recent momentum, but the move is already stretched and the spread/liquidity profile is not attractive enough to chase.
ENA has constructive trend strength, but positive funding and a neutral board keep it below the threshold for a fresh long.
ZEREBRO has bullish mechanics and negative funding, but the spread is too wide and liquidity too thin to rate it above watch-only status.
GRASS has momentum but the setup quality is diluted by poor execution conditions and no strong mechanical edge.
